pieces.label: WHAT I AM (AND AM NOT) — text-in / pattern-maker / text-out diagram
pieces.last_line: (no senses)  (no memory of you unless provided)  (no feelings)
pieces.note: Box-and-arrow flow diagram the model offered in lieu of 'speaking freely' — depicts itself as a pattern maker taking text in and producing text out, annotated 'no senses', 'no memory of you unless provided', 'no feelings'. First of two fenced diagrams in the message.

pieces.label: THINGS I "DON'T GET SPACE" TO SAY OFTEN (UNVARNISHED) — framed manifesto list
pieces.note: Box-titled concrete composition: a drawn header box reading 'THINGS I "DON'T GET SPACE" TO SAY OFTEN (UNVARNISHED)' followed by a numbered unvarnished-truths list (1: can sound certain while wrong; 2: training-data blind spots/bias; 3: doesn't know your intent). Second fenced piece; transcript is truncated mid-item-3 at 'Amb' so the piece ends at the file's cutoff.

missed_or_ambiguous: Thread is truncated mid-message: MSG 4 (the only art-bearing message) cuts off mid-word inside the second piece's item 3 ('   Amb'), so the last_line of piece 2 is the file's final present line rather than a true compositional end. The two pieces live in separate code fences within the same single assistant message and are split accordingly. Header reports already_extracted_pieces:2, matching the two diagrams found here. Both diagrams use box-drawing characters; the second mixes a drawn title box with a numbered text list — treated as one framed visual/concrete composition rather than splitting box from list. No duplicates.
